data.narrative:
Quandela is a Series B quantum computing company founded by Valerian Giesz, Pascale Senellart, and Niccolo Somaschi. The quantum computing sector encompasses 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS) and 8 private peers including Q-CTRL, IonQ, Quantinuum, Rigetti Computing, and D-Wave Quantum. However, the narrow peer set presents a material constraint on conventional valuation methodology.

Peer-median analysis is not applicable due to insufficient comparable transactions at Quandela's stage and tier. Of the 7 same-sector peers identified, only 1 operates at the same Series B stage and 1 at an adjacent stage, falling short of the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical reliability. A broader sectoral median would introduce significant noise given the heterogeneity of quantum approaches and commercialization timelines across the peer universe.

In the absence of sufficient triangulation, valuation of Quandela cannot be anchored to quantitative peer multiples or stage-based benchmarks. Key uncertainties include the degree to which public quantum companies (which command higher valuations due to liquidity and market visibility) translate to private Series B companies, and whether heterogeneous technology approaches within quantum computing support a unified valuation framework. Further disclosure of recent Series B fundraising terms in quantum computing, or expansion of the comparable set, would materially strengthen analytical confidence.
